```{r} library(tidyverse) library(tidyselect) library(tibble) library(lubridate) library(sf) library(tigris) library(lubridate) library(tidygeocoder) library(gt) library(gtsummary) # nice count visualizations library(forcats) # a specific data wrangling issue library(readxl) ``` THIS IS STILL A MAJOR WORK IN PROGRESS. I JUST HAVE THE RAW FORMAT HERE. > I need to add Canadian trips to my dataset and exclude them in the United States portion (or maybe incorporate? I don't know at the moment) # The United States ```{r, output = FALSE} # data that I type in manually daniel_travel_map <- readxl::read_xlsx("trip_data.xlsx") |> # I can also add coordinate information here... maybe play around with that? mutate(start_date = as_date(start_date), end_date = as_date(end_date)) # US states shapefile us <- states(cb = TRUE) |> shift_geometry() #for alaska and hawaii (and PR) ``` These visualizations do not take into account smaller trips within the state I live in (e.g. travelling from Allston to Boston doesn't count, but something like Haverhill to Fall River counts). These are just ones I thought were memorable. Thus, I don't count just passing through a state. I also don't count states I have lived in; that will probably be a separate visualization. ```{r} # Count visit_counts <- daniel_travel_map |> count(state, name = "visits") # Join counts to map map_data <- us |> left_join( visit_counts, by=c("NAME" = "state") ) |> mutate( visits=ifelse(is.na(visits), 0, visits) ) # Plot ggplot(map_data) + geom_sf(aes(fill = visits)) + coord_sf(xlim = c(-3500000, 3500000), ylim=c(-2500000, 2500000), expand=FALSE) + #stupid fix by playing around with numbers scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Trips") + scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high="blue") + theme_minimal() + labs(title = "How many times have I made significant travel to this state?") ``` 2. Timeline on unique states I have been to (manually add states I lived in like California), and on the X axis, show the state name. > Still a couple of fixes I want here since it's not 100% accurate but it's fine ```{r} # first, count unique states from the earliest I've been there first_travel <- daniel_travel_map |> group_by(state) |> filter(start_date == min(start_date)) |> # somehow this worked lol rename(first_visit_date = start_date) |> ungroup(state) |> mutate(states_visited_order = nrow(first_travel) - row_number() + 1) #again, stupid fix but it works # now we can plot it ggplot(data = first_travel, aes(x = first_visit_date, y=states_visited_order)) + geom_step() + geom_text(aes(label = state), nudge_x=-225, size=3) + theme_bw() + labs(x = "Date of Travel", y="Number of States", title="States visited for the first time") ``` 3. Small multiples visualization of states I have visited each year + lived in (e.g. 2025 will have MA and NY as "lived" and RI, TX as travelled) > I think this is more interesting in my head ```{r} ``` 3.5. Similar version but with a lineplot ordered by when the state joined the union (this wouldn't be the exact thing) > This will allow me to see when I visited each state ANd how many times ```{r} ggplot(data = daniel_travel_map, aes(x = start_date, y=state)) + geom_line() ``` 4. Word cloud of my travel descriptions ```{r} daniel_travel_map |> dplyr::select(desc) ``` 5. Descriptive statistics: how many redeyes, how many business vs leisure trips, distribution of days I stayed (faceted by something), average length of trip by days, number of day trips, average distance of trips (MAYBE) etc. ```{r} ``` 6. Capital city list: Have I been to the state capital? ```{r} ``` 7. Lines of travel (How have I travelled between each state?): May not work if I don't have proper coordinates. Maybe I can do it based on thickness? 8. A county map? Includes places I've just passed by (do this manually): something approximate.
